sections.paragraph: m., elation, pride (Arab. ثَخْصُ; Talmud. שַׁחַץ id.), Job 28:8 41:26 , בְּנֵי שַׁחַץ Vulg. filii superbiæ, i.e. the larger ravenous beasts, as the lion; so called from the pride of walking. Lions is the rendering of the Chaldee intp., chap. 28; but the other passage demands the above wider signification. Compare Bochart, Hieroz. I., p. 718.
